identificationModule.briefTitle: A Phase 1/2, Open-label, Safety and Dosing Study of Autologous CART Cells (Desmoglein 3 Chimeric Autoantibody Receptor T Cells [DSG3-CAART] or CD19-specific Chimeric Antigen Receptor T Cells [CABA-201]) in Subjects With Active, Pemphigus Vulgaris (RESET-PV)
identificationModule.officialTitle: A Phase 1/2, Open-label, Safety and Dosing Study of Autologous CART Cells (Desmoglein 3 Chimeric Autoantibody Receptor T Cells [DSG3-CAART] or CD19-specific Chimeric Antigen Receptor T Cells [CABA-201]) in Subjects With Active, Pemphigus Vulgaris

descriptionModule.briefSummary: A phase 1/2, open-label, safety and dosing study of autologous CART cells (desmoglein 3 chimeric autoantibody receptor T cells \[DSG3-CAART\] or CD19-specific Chimeric Antigen Receptor T cells \[CABA-201\]) in subjects with active, pemphigus vulgaris
descriptionModule.detailedDescription:
Pemphigus vulgaris (PV) is a B-cell mediated autoimmune disorder in which painful blisters are formed on the skin or mucosal membrane, including the mouth, nose, throat, eyelids, anus, and genitals.

This phase 1/2 study is being conducted in two parts. The first part is the main study conducted to find the maximum tolerated dose and optimal fractionated infusion schedule of an investigational cell therapy, DSG3-CAART, that can be given to patients with mucosal PV who are inadequately managed by standard therapies. This study is closed to enrollment.

The second part is a sub-study is being conducted to investigate if CABA-201, also called resecabtagene autoleucel, or "rese-cel", can be safely administered while achieving clinical responses without the need for preconditioning in mucosal-dominant PV (mPV) and mucocutaneous PV (mcPV) patients. This sub-study is open to enrollment.

DSG3-CAART or CABA-201 may potentially lead to complete and durable remission of disease.

armGroups.label: DSG3-CAART
armGroups.description:
Single or multiple intravenous infusion(s) of DSG3-CAART at varying dose levels.

This study is now closed to enrollment.

armGroups.label: CABA-201
armGroups.description:
Infusion of CABA-201 with or without cyclophosphamide and fludarabine preconditioning, or with or without cyclophosphamide preconditioning.

This sub-study is open to enrollment.

interventions.name: DSG3-CAART
interventions.description: Intravenous infusions of DSG3-CAART alone at different doses and different fractionations, with or without intravenous immunoglobulin, cyclophosphamide, and/or fludarabine.

interventions.name: CABA-201
interventions.description: Single intravenous infusion of CABA-201 at escalating doses, with or without preconditioning.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Adverse events, including Dose Limit Toxicity
primaryOutcomes.description: Incidence of adverse events that are related to DSG3-CAART therapy

primaryOutcomes.measure: For CABA-201 Sub-study: To evaluate adverse events reported by subjects
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Up to 28 days after CABA-201 infusion

secondaryOutcomes.description: Percent of total cells for infusion that are CAAR-transduced cells by flow cytometry
Total DSG3-CAART positive cells for each manufacturing run by flow cytometry
Cellular kinetics profile of DSG3-CAART assessed by quantitative polymerase chain reaction
Change in DSG3 autoantibody titer by ELISA compared to pre-infusion visit
Proportion of subjects achieving serologic remission, determined by negative DSG3 ELISA titer
Change in PDAI compared to pre-infusion visit, scored on a 0-250 scale where a greater number represents more disease activity

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Clinical remission: complete remission off therapy and complete remission on minimal therapy
secondaryOutcomes.description: Proportion of subjects achieving complete remission, determined by a PDAI activity score of 0 for at least 2 months, either off therapy or on minimal therapy

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Time to clinical remission and time to serologic remission
secondaryOutcomes.description: Time to clinical remission and time to serologic remission from the last infusion

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Duration of clinical remission and duration of serologic remission
secondaryOutcomes.description: Duration of clinical remission and duration of serologic remission sustained after achieving the initial remission

secondaryOutcomes.measure: For CABA-201 Sub-study: To evaluate adverse events reported by subjects
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Up to 156 weeks after CABA-201 infusion

secondaryOutcomes.measure: For CABA-201 Sub-study: To characterize the pharmacodynamics (PD)
secondaryOutcomes.description: Levels of B cells in the blood

secondaryOutcomes.measure: For CABA-201 Sub-study: To characterize the pharmacokinetics (PK)
secondaryOutcomes.description: Levels of CABA-201-positive T cells in the blood

secondaryOutcomes.measure: For CABA-201 Sub-study: To evaluate autoantibody -related biomarkers
secondaryOutcomes.description: Levels of serum anti-DSG3 and anti-DSG1 antibodies

secondaryOutcomes.measure: For CABA-201 Sub-study: To evaluate efficacy
secondaryOutcomes.description: Absolute and percent change in disease activity by Pemphigus Disease Area Index (PDAI)

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria for DSG3-CAART: Closed to enrollment

* Confirmed diagnosis of mPV by prior or screening biopsy and prior positive anti- DSG3 antibody ELISA
* mPV inadequately managed by at least one standard immunosuppressive therapies
* Active mPV at screening
* Anti-DSG3 antibody ELISA positive at screening

Inclusion Criteria for CABA-201 sub-study: Open to enrollment

* Age ≥18
* Confirmed diagnosis of PV by prior or screening biopsy and prior positive DSG3 ELISA, IIF, and/or DIF
* PV inadequately managed by at least one standard immunosuppressive therapy
* Active PV at screening
* DSG3 ELISA positive at screening

Exclusion Criteria:

* Active cutaneous lesions associated with PV that indicates mucocutaneous rather than mucosal-dominant disease
* Rituximab in last 12 months unless PV symptoms have recently worsened or anti-DSG3 antibody titers have recently increased
* Prednisone \> 0.25mg/kg/day
* Other autoimmune disorder requiring immunosuppressive therapies
* Investigational treatment in last 3 months

Exclusion Criteria for CABA-201 sub-study

* Have paraneoplastic pemphigus or active malignancy (not including non-melanoma skin cancer) or malignancy diagnosed within the previous 5 years
* Have received rituximab or other anti-CD20 or anti-CD19 therapies in last 12 months unless anti-DSG3 antibody titers have recently increased or PV symptoms have recently worsened
* Prednisone \> 0.25mg/kg/day
* Other autoimmune disorder requiring immunosuppressive therapies
* Treatment with any investigational agent within 4 weeks or 5 half-lives, whichever is longer.

locations.facility: Mount Sinai - Icahn School of Medicine
University of North Carolina, Department of Dermatology
UT Southwestern Medical Center, Dept. of Dermatology

references.citation: Shu J, Xie W, Chen Z, Offringa R, Hu Y, Mei H. The enchanting canvas of CAR technology: Unveiling its wonders in non-neoplastic diseases. Med. 2024 Jun 14;5(6):495-529. doi: 10.1016/j.medj.2024.03.016. Epub 2024 Apr 11.
